title: HEXm — Management levers
subtitle: Shop vs network average
legend_thresholds: thresholds: good ≥ network %good% % · danger < %danger% % · reversed for costs
k_tickets_day_what: Number of till receipts per day (POS count).
k_tickets_day_why: Direct footfall measure: the base of all turnover — no visits, no sales.
k_google_what: Average rating of the Google Business profile.
k_google_why: First attraction filter: local reputation decides who walks in.
k_new_clients_what: Number of new identified customers (loyalty/CRM) per month.
k_new_clients_why: Ability to widen the customer base: the growth engine of traffic over time.
k_loyalty_rate_what: Customers with ≥ 2 visits ÷ total customers, over the period.
k_loyalty_rate_why: Heart of recurrence: keeping a customer costs far less than recruiting one.
k_basket_why: Value created per visit: rises when the customer returns and buys more.
k_members_what: Monthly change in the number of loyalty programme members.
k_members_why: Leading indicator of recurrence: a growing loyalty base = recurring revenue.
k_mystery_what: Mystery shopper audit grid, scored out of 100.
k_mystery_why: Objective, external measure of the experience actually lived in the shop.
k_haccp_what: Monthly HACCP audit, scored out of 100 (zero tolerance).
k_haccp_why: Non-negotiable food safety: the base of trust and legal compliance.
k_wait_what: Average time (sec) before a customer is attended to.
k_wait_why: First signal of perceived service quality: beyond 10 s the experience degrades.
k_food_why: First variable cost item: lever no. 1 of product margin.
k_gross_why: Product profitability after materials: what remains to cover everything else.
k_waste_what: Value of waste + unsold items ÷ turnover.
k_waste_why: Avoidable losses: reveals overstocking and aligns production with real sales.
k_labour_why: Second cost item: drives productivity and the balance of the model.
k_ca_hour_why: Hourly productivity: the base for sizing the schedule and staffing.
k_prod_slot_what: Turnover generated ÷ worked hours, per time slot.
k_prod_slot_why: Aligns the schedule precisely with footfall peaks: no over/under-staffing.
k_ca_m2_why: Space efficiency: rent is a major fixed cost that must pay off.
k_week_evo_what: (Week turnover − same week N-1) ÷ N-1 turnover.
k_week_evo_why: Growth dynamic: rising turnover mechanically dilutes fixed costs.
k_payback_what: Total investment ÷ total annual gross margin (= payback in years).
k_payback_why: Structural financial health: in how many years the investment pays back.
